task: I’m dealing with a delivery that got delayed, and the other side is asking to adjust the paperwork before it moves forward. What’s a straightforward way to respond so the record stays clear if there’s any follow-up later?

turns.agent_response:
A straightforward way is to keep it factual, neutral, and written, so there’s a clear record of what was requested and agreed.

You could say:

> We understand the delivery has been delayed. To keep the record accurate, please send the requested paperwork changes in writing, and we’ll review them before anything moves forward. We want to make sure all documentation reflects the current status clearly in case there are any follow-up questions later.

If you want, I can also help you draft a firmer version or a more diplomatic one depending on the situation.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but I’d want it tighter. Too much soft language.

Make it more like:

> The delivery is delayed. If any paperwork needs to change before release, send the revised terms in writing so the record matches the actual arrangement. We will review the updated documents before any further action.

I’d want it to say, plain, that nothing moves until the paperwork is right. That’s the part that protects you later.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version:

> The delivery is delayed. If any paperwork needs to be changed before release, send the revised terms in writing so the record reflects the actual arrangement. No further action will be taken until the updated documents are reviewed and confirmed.

If you want, I can make it even firmer or more formal.

user_simulator.text: That works. Tight enough. The “no further action will be taken” part is the point. Keeps it clean if somebody tries to argue later.
